project_structure: pnpm monorepo with packages/cli (citty-based command surface), packages/server (MCP server + Hono dashboard), packages/shared (Zod schemas + i18n + atomic-write). The install pipeline lives entirely in packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts (~1665 LOC). Call chain: runInitCommand(args) -> resolveInitCliIntent (args -> InitOptions) -> buildInitExecutionPlan -> buildInitFabricPlan (calls planFreshPath × 3 — this is the BLOCKING throw site) -> [wizard?] -> executeInitExecutionPlan -> for each step: preflight | scaffold (executeInitFabricPlan) | bootstrap (skill+hook templates via runBestEffort) | mcp | hooks (installHooks) | post-setup. Per-template idempotency for skills/hooks/sections is already implemented at packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts via copyTextIdempotent (read-then-byte-compare-then-skip) and mergeJsonIdempotent (deep merge + jsonEqual). The throw-on-existing guard is ONLY on the three .fabric core files (agents.meta.json, events.jsonl, forensic.json) at the scaffold stage; bootstrap/hooks/mcp stages already tolerate re-runs.

relevant_files.rationale: The primary modification target. Contains planFreshPath (L1020-L1030) — the throw site that makes install non-idempotent — plus the entire state machine: runInitCommand (L307), resolveInitCliIntent (L477), buildInitExecutionPlan (L514), buildInitFabricPlan (L623) where planFreshPath is called 3× for metaPath/eventsPath/forensicPath, executeInitExecutionPlan (L558), executeInitFabricPlan (L669). Also contains shouldReplaceWritableDirectory (L1004) which throws on the same condition for the .fabric directory when it is a non-directory file.

key_code.description: The throw site. Returns 'created' when path is absent, 'overwritten' when options.force is true, otherwise throws t('cli.install.errors.abort-existing', {path}). This is what Bug V and Bug Z both hit. Called 3× from buildInitFabricPlan for agents.meta.json, events.jsonl, forensic.json — so any one of those existing triggers the throw.

Async planner that produces InitScaffoldPlan. Calls planFreshPath at L641-L643 BEFORE any execution branch is taken. This is why --plan (planOnly) cannot work on an existing workspace: the planFreshPath throws fire during plan construction, not at write time. Bug Z root cause.

Executes the plan step-by-step. The planOnly branch is at L571-L579 — it prints the preview and returns WITHOUT touching disk. But this branch is unreachable on an existing workspace because the planFreshPath throw fires during the upstream buildInitFabricPlan call. Refactor must move existing-state detection out of planning into a non-throwing classification step.

Actual file writes. Already has per-file idempotency contracts: isReapply preserves events.jsonl byte-identically (L726-L735), AGENTS.md is only written when absent (L692-L694), fabric-config.json written only when absent via writeDefaultFabricConfig (L395-L475), knowledge subdirs use mkdirSync recursive (always safe). Only agents.meta.json (L722) and forensic.json (L738) unconditionally overwrite via atomicWriteJson — these need diff-mode classification.

Sibling guard to planFreshPath. Returns false if path absent OR is a directory; throws on non-force when path is a file in directory's place. Currently only invoked for fabricDir at L639. Need to verify behavior under diff-mode — likely keep this as a 'drift detected' signal because a file where .fabric/ should be is definitionally user-modification, not canonical-missing-piece.

Flag-to-options mapper. Currently maps args.reapply=true => options.force=true (L485) — so --reapply implicitly bypasses planFreshPath via force. This conflation is exactly what the rc.14 plan wants to dissolve: diff-mode should be the default, and --force / --reapply become escape-hatches (or get removed in rc.15).

Companion to planFreshPath. mkdirs parent, then if action === 'overwritten' AND path exists, rmSync recursive force. Diff-mode replaces 'overwritten' with finer-grained verbs (missing → write, present-canonical → skip, drifted → abort).

Currently only fires under --reapply (executeInitFabricPlan L755-L759), event_type='reapply_completed'. Diff-mode should append a new event_type (e.g. 'install_diff_applied' / 'install_no_op') for every install run, ledger preserved, to give doctor/forensic visibility into idempotent re-runs.

Top-level orchestrator. Notable: L312-L316 enforces lock-check ONLY for --reapply, not for default install. If diff-mode becomes the default, lock-check (checkLockOrThrow) should fire for every install on an already-initialized workspace because a running serve has open handles on events.jsonl.

relevant_files.topic_relation: install-diff-state perspective: every state-machine vertex (fresh empty / already-init / drifted / user-modified) is decided inside this one file. The refactor surface is contained — no cross-package signatures change. Per-file detection strategy lives at buildInitFabricPlan; execution-vs-planning split happens at executeInitExecutionPlan; flag semantics live at resolveInitCliIntent.

relevant_files.rationale: Already implements the diff-mode pattern we need to lift up to the scaffold stage. copyTextIdempotent (L586-L604) reads existing file, byte-compares to source template, skips on match, overwrites on mismatch. mergeJsonIdempotent (L606-L620) deep-merges + jsonEqual check + skip-or-write. These are reference implementations for the new state classification — bootstrap and hooks stages are already naturally idempotent; the scaffold stage just needs the same treatment.

key_code.description: Reference pattern for byte-compare detection. If existsSync(target), readFileSync, compare to source; if identical => status 'skipped' message 'up-to-date'; else atomicWriteText. This is exactly the 'present-canonical' vs 'drifted' detection diff-mode needs. Returns InstallStepResult with status='written'|'skipped'|'error' — already structurally compatible with a diff-mode result schema.

Reference pattern for merge-based idempotency (MCP configs, hook configs). deepMerge with arrayAppendPaths, then jsonEqual(existing, merged) — if equal, skip. Critical insight: MCP configs and hook configs are NOT byte-compared, they are MERGED. Diff-mode at the .codex/config.toml / .claude/settings.json / .cursor/mcp.json level cannot use byte-compare; the canonical state is 'fabric block deep-merges in cleanly'. User edits to other keys are non-drift.

Managed-section pattern. Replaces only the region between FABRIC_SECTION_BEGIN_MARKER and FABRIC_SECTION_END_MARKER. Outside the markers is 100% user-owned. The diff detection compares next === existing (L562) and skips if equal. This is the third detection model: 'managed region byte-compare' (distinct from 'whole-file byte-compare' and 'merged-blob structural-compare').

relevant_files.topic_relation: install-diff-state perspective: this file already solves the diff-mode problem for ~80% of install's write surface (skills, hook scripts, hook configs, MCP configs, managed sections). The remaining 20% — agents.meta.json, events.jsonl, forensic.json — are the only files still throwing. The refactor is essentially 'apply the existing idempotent-write pattern to the three scaffold-stage files'.

relevant_files.rationale: Defines the current contract for the throw-on-existing behavior. Test 'rejects an already-initialized project when --force is not set' (L33-L40) directly asserts initFabric throws. Test 'error thrown on existing v2.0 fabric meta file mentions the conflicting file path' (L42-L59) asserts the error message format. These tests MUST be rewritten under diff-mode: the no-force re-run becomes a no-op success (not a throw), and the error path becomes 'drift detected' (still throws, but on a different condition).

key_code.description: Asserts initFabric throws on re-run without --force. Under diff-mode this becomes the OPPOSITE assertion: re-run with no flags should succeed as a no-op (or apply missing pieces). The drift case becomes a NEW test: 'rejects when canonical file has user modification'.

Already validate byte-identical agents.meta.json + events.jsonl prefix across two --reapply runs. Diff-mode generalises this — the SAME assertion should hold for plain `fab install` re-runs, no flag required. These tests are likely repurposable as-is by dropping the {reapply:true, force:true} options object.

relevant_files.topic_relation: install-diff-state perspective: the current contract test makes the bleeding explicit. Bug V (idempotency) violates this test's premise (throw on re-run) by making re-run the happy path. The fixture pattern (createWerewolfFixtureRoot, writeFixtureFile) is the test scaffolding the rc.14 plan will reuse for the new state-detection cases (missing-piece-applied, drift-aborted, dry-run-on-existing-succeeds).

relevant_files.rationale: Surface-level tests for install CLI flags including --reapply behavior (line 47 'treats --reapply as a canonical forceful rerun') and the fabric-config.json field list contract. Asserts current --reapply semantics; if diff-mode kills the implicit --force coupling at install.ts L485 (reapply ? true : args.force), these tests will need updating to reflect the new semantics.

key_code.description: Enumerates the fabric-config.json fields that writeDefaultFabricConfig must emit. Important for diff-mode because if the canonical default set grows (rc.9+ added 11 new fields), an existing config from an older install would 'look drifted' simply by missing fields. The diff-mode detector for fabric-config must check 'is fabric_language present' rather than 'are all fields present', to avoid false-drift on legitimately old configs.

relevant_files.topic_relation: install-diff-state perspective: highlights a subtle drift-detection trap — schema growth over time means 'old canonical' looks identical to 'user-modified'. The detection strategy must distinguish version-skew (acceptable, auto-migrate or accept-as-is) from content-tamper (abort).

relevant_files.rationale: Integration tests for the per-file idempotent installers (skills + hooks). Validates the copyTextIdempotent / mergeJsonIdempotent patterns end-to-end across .claude/, .codex/, .cursor/ directories. Reference for how the rc.14 refactor should assert state-machine transitions on the three remaining throw-prone files.
relevant_files.topic_relation: install-diff-state perspective: shows the testing shape for already-idempotent install steps — assertions cover written/skipped/error step results across multiple runs. The new tests for diff-mode agents.meta.json/events.jsonl/forensic.json should mirror this shape.

relevant_files.rationale: Produces the forensic.json content via buildForensicReport (called at install.ts L645). Important for diff-mode because forensic.json is REGENERATED every install (it's a snapshot of the workspace, not user state), so 'drift detection' on forensic.json is semantically meaningless — the canonical action under diff-mode is always 'rewrite with latest snapshot'. This file's role is informational/snapshot, not configuration; classification differs from agents.meta.json.
relevant_files.topic_relation: install-diff-state perspective: forensic.json is the file with the LEAST drift-detection value — its semantic contract is 'last known repo state at install time'. The diff-mode plan can treat it as 'always rewrite, never drift-check' (similar to a build artifact). This is one of three distinct file semantics in the install write surface.

relevant_files.rationale: Hosts the error message string cli.install.errors.abort-existing (L210) currently thrown by planFreshPath. Diff-mode introduces new user-facing messages: 'applying N missing pieces', 'drift detected at <path>, edit detected — re-run with --force to overwrite or `fab doctor` to inspect', 'workspace already canonical — no-op'. These need i18n keys in both en.ts and zh-CN.ts.

key_code.description: Current error: 'ABORT: {path} already exists. fab install is non-destructive.' Under diff-mode this gets replaced with a drift-specific message and supplemented with success messages for the no-op and apply-missing-pieces happy paths.

relevant_files.topic_relation: install-diff-state perspective: the messaging contract is part of the UX surface. The 'helpful message on drift' requirement in the plan maps directly to new i18n keys here. Must update both en.ts and zh-CN.ts atomically.

relevant_files.rationale: AgentsMeta type lives in @fenglimg/fabric-shared (imported at install.ts L8). The diff-detector for agents.meta.json must compare a parsed AgentsMeta structure against the canonical createInitialMeta() output (install.ts L1314-L1324). Need to check if AgentsMeta has fields that legitimately change between init runs (e.g. revision, counters) — if so, structural comparison must mask those fields.
relevant_files.topic_relation: install-diff-state perspective: defines the schema by which agents.meta.json drift is detected. The detection rule cannot be raw byte-compare because revision='sha256:initial' would invalidate after the first scan run.

relevant_files.rationale: scan.ts hosts detectExistingLanguage (imported at install.ts L19) and runInitScan (called at install.ts L746 during scaffold). runInitScan is what populates agents.meta.json with nodes/counters after the initial empty write. This is why agents.meta.json starts as {nodes:{}, counters:{...zero}} but is immediately mutated by the scan stage — a naive byte-compare for drift detection on agents.meta.json would always 'detect drift' immediately after install.
relevant_files.topic_relation: install-diff-state perspective: surfaces a critical detection nuance — agents.meta.json's canonical state immediately after install is NOT the canonical state on second run. The 'is this canonical?' check must permit any state derivable from runInitScan applied to the workspace, not strictly the empty initial meta.

relevant_files.rationale: Source-of-truth for byte-comparable hook templates (fabric-hint.cjs, knowledge-hint-broad.cjs, knowledge-hint-narrow.cjs). These ARE byte-comparable for drift detection because copyTextIdempotent at skills-and-hooks.ts L586 already does exactly that — so the diff-mode logic for hook scripts is essentially already in place. No new logic needed here, but tests should sanity-check that template content didn't drift from what's deployed.
relevant_files.topic_relation: install-diff-state perspective: example of the 'easy' file class — byte-stable template, byte-stable destination, simple readFileSync + === comparison handles drift detection completely. Distinct from the 'merged JSON' class (MCP configs) and the 'mutated by other stages' class (agents.meta.json).

patterns: Three distinct idempotency patterns coexist in the install pipeline today: (1) Byte-compare-then-skip — used by copyTextIdempotent for skill SKILL.md files and hook .cjs scripts (skills-and-hooks.ts L586-L604). Reads target, compares to source template string, skips if equal, atomicWriteText otherwise. (2) Deep-merge-then-jsonEqual — used by mergeJsonIdempotent for .claude/settings.json, .codex/config.toml, .cursor/mcp.json (L606-L620). Loads existing JSON, deepMerges fabric fragment with arrayAppendPaths semantics, compares pre-merge vs post-merge; skips if no change. Critical: user keys outside the fabric block are preserved, so drift detection here is 'does the fabric block deep-merge in cleanly' not 'is the file byte-identical'. (3) Managed-section regex-replace — used by addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection for CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / .cursor/rules (L513-L580). Operates between FABRIC_SECTION_BEGIN_MARKER and FABRIC_SECTION_END_MARKER; content outside markers is 100% user-owned and untouched. (4) The OUTLIER — planFreshPath at install.ts L1020 — uses a fourth pattern: 'throw unless --force'. This is the inconsistency the rc.14 refactor removes. The diff-mode replacement should follow pattern (1) for agents.meta.json (with field-level masking for revision/counters), 'always-rewrite' for forensic.json (it's a snapshot), and 'preserve verbatim, never overwrite' for events.jsonl (the --reapply branch at L726-L735 already implements this).
dependencies: install.ts external deps: @fenglimg/fabric-shared (AgentsMeta type, defaultAgentsMetaCounters, atomicWriteJson, atomicWriteText), @fenglimg/fabric-server (checkLockOrThrow), citty (defineCommand for CLI surface), @clack/prompts (interactive wizard). Internal deps: ./scan.ts (detectExistingLanguage, runInitScan, ResolvedLanguage), ./config.ts (installMcpClients), ./hooks.ts (installHooks), ../scanner/forensic.ts (buildForensicReport), ../config/json.ts (ClaudeMcpScope type), ../config/resolver.ts (detectClientSupports, DetectedClientSupport), ../install/skills-and-hooks.ts (8 skill/hook helpers + readFabricLanguagePreference + InstallStepResult), ../i18n.ts (t), ../colors.ts, ../dev-mode.ts. Diff-mode refactor changes no public type signatures of install.ts exports — runInitCommand, buildInitFabricPlan, buildInitExecutionPlan, executeInitExecutionPlan, executeInitFabricPlan, initFabric all keep their signatures; only the THROW behavior of planFreshPath becomes a CLASSIFICATION returning a richer InitWriteAction enum (currently 'created' | 'overwritten'; needs 'present-canonical' | 'drifted' | 'missing' to express the new state-machine).
integration_points: Throw-on-existing entry points (the bleeding sites): packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts:1014 (shouldReplaceWritableDirectory for .fabric/ as file-not-dir), packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts:1026 (planFreshPath for any of metaPath/eventsPath/forensicPath). Both throw the same i18n key cli.install.errors.abort-existing. Call sites of planFreshPath: install.ts:641 (metaPath), install.ts:642 (eventsPath), install.ts:643 (forensicPath) — all inside buildInitFabricPlan. Plan-only check (currently unreachable on existing workspace): install.ts:571-579 inside executeInitExecutionPlan — the planOnly branch returns the buildPlanOnlyScaffoldResult preview but is GATED behind the buildInitFabricPlan throw. Force coupling: install.ts:485 (resolveInitCliIntent collapses reapply=true into force=true). Lock-check site: install.ts:314-316 (currently --reapply-only; diff-mode default should extend to all install runs on existing workspace). Ledger event append: install.ts:755-759 (appendReapplyLedgerEvent, currently --reapply-only; diff-mode should emit per-run for visibility into no-op vs apply-missing-pieces vs drift-aborted outcomes). Hook + skill idempotent installers (already-correct pattern): packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts:586 (copyTextIdempotent), :606 (mergeJsonIdempotent), :513 (addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection). Test surface: packages/cli/__tests__/integration/init-guard.test.ts (I2 + I3 contracts will be rewritten under diff-mode), packages/cli/__tests__/install-cli-surface.test.ts (--reapply tests), packages/cli/__tests__/integration/install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts (per-file idempotency reference shape).
constraints: C1 (Bug Z root cause): buildInitFabricPlan calls planFreshPath synchronously, BEFORE executeInitExecutionPlan checks planOnly. Any --plan invocation on an existing workspace throws during plan-construction, never reaching the planOnly print branch. Refactor must split state-classification (non-throwing, runs always) from state-transition (throwing or writing, gated by mode). C2 (events.jsonl is append-only): contains user history. Under diff-mode default, ANY existing events.jsonl is canonical-by-presence and must be preserved verbatim — the --reapply branch at L726-L735 already enforces this; diff-mode should make it the default. Truncating events.jsonl loses ledger history. C3 (agents.meta.json mutates post-install): runInitScan (called from executeInitFabricPlan L746) mutates agents.meta.json immediately after install writes the empty version. A pure byte-compare drift detector would treat the post-scan state as 'drifted' on every second install. Detection must either run BEFORE scan or compare against the post-scan canonical (probably easier: detect 'is this a valid AgentsMeta structure with our revision schema' rather than 'is this byte-identical to createInitialMeta()'). C4 (forensic.json is a snapshot): regenerated from buildForensicReport every run. 'Drift detection' on it is semantically void — the canonical action is always 'regenerate'. C5 (fabric-config.json schema grows): rc.9 added 11 fields, rc.12 changed fabric_language fixation. Old configs are missing fields but are still 'canonical' for their era. Drift detector must not penalize version-skew. The writeDefaultFabricConfig early-return (L397: if existsSync, return) already handles this conservatively — do not migrate fields. C6 (--force / --reapply backward-compat): per plan-text, rc.14 keeps them as escape-hatches; rc.15 removes them. Refactor must thread them through without breaking the I3 reapply-idempotency tests. C7 (--reapply currently sets force=true): install.ts:485. This conflation should be broken — diff-mode --force means 'skip drift check and overwrite' while --reapply means 'regenerate even if canonical'. They are semantically distinct under diff-mode. C8 (lock-check coverage): checkLockOrThrow currently fires only for --reapply (L314). Under diff-mode-default, any install on an existing workspace might write to events.jsonl (ledger event), so the lock-check needs to expand to default install runs, with --force as the legitimate bypass (matching its current --reapply-path semantic).

clarification_needs.question: What is the diff-mode action when a user has byte-edited agents.meta.json (e.g. hand-edited a counter)?
clarification_needs.context: agents.meta.json is normally MCP-managed (rule-meta sync + scan). Direct user edits are rare but possible. Options shape the error-vs-recover behavior on drift.
options: Abort with drift message + suggest `fab doctor` to inspect (strict)
Abort with drift message + suggest `fab install --force` to overwrite
Auto-merge: regenerate canonical structure but preserve user counter values (lenient)
Treat any well-formed AgentsMeta as canonical, only abort on parse failure

clarification_needs.question: Should `fab install` (no flags) on a fully-canonical existing workspace be a silent no-op or print a 'workspace already canonical' confirmation?
clarification_needs.context: UX trade-off: silent is the Unix way; explicit is better discovery for users new to Fabric. The plan says 'detect existing state, auto-apply missing pieces' — unclear if the all-canonical case is summarized or silent.
options: Print one-line confirmation: 'Workspace already canonical (N files verified)'
Print full state table showing each file status: canonical / missing / drifted

clarification_needs.question: Under diff-mode default, should `--reapply` semantics keep the 'preserve events.jsonl + append reapply_completed ledger event' behavior, or merge into a single diff-mode flow that emits its own ledger event type per run?
clarification_needs.context: Currently --reapply has a unique ledger event (reapply_completed). Diff-mode default would naturally want a ledger event too (e.g. install_diff_applied with payload describing which files were missing-applied vs canonical-verified). Question: do we keep --reapply's event distinct or unify under diff-mode?
options: Keep --reapply event distinct; diff-mode emits install_diff_applied with payload
Unify: diff-mode emits install_completed with mode={'fresh'|'apply-missing'|'noop'|'reapply'} field
Drop the reapply event in rc.14 (since --reapply is slated for removal in rc.15 anyway), emit only install_diff_applied

clarification_needs.question: Should the new InitWriteAction enum extend the existing 'created' | 'overwritten' values, or be a separate diff-mode classification?
clarification_needs.context: Current InitWriteAction is consumed by formatInitPathAction (L1608) for user output and by preparePlannedPath (L1032). Adding 'present-canonical' / 'drifted' / 'missing' extends the formatter's switch. Alternative: introduce a separate DiffState type and translate to InitWriteAction at the rendering boundary.
options: Extend InitWriteAction with new variants (one type, exhaustive switch)
Introduce DiffFileState separately, translate at render-time (clean separation of concerns)
Replace InitWriteAction entirely with the new DiffFileState (breaking, requires snapshot update)

_metadata.task_description: Refactor `fab install` to be naturally idempotent via diff-mode. Current `planFreshPath` (packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts:1020) throws on already-init workspace unless `--force`. New behavior: detect existing state, auto-apply missing pieces, abort with helpful message on drift/user-modification. `--dry-run` must work on any workspace state. Bug V (idempotency) and Bug Z (dry-run on existing workspace fails) share root cause.
